content.problemStatement:
There are **N** special points on the 2-D plane. You can travel from a special point (**xi**, **yi**) to another special point (**xj**, **yj**) if and only if |**xi** - **xj**| + |**yi** - **yj**| ≥ **D**.

You need to find the largest integer **D**, such that we can start at some special point and visit each of the other special points, directly or through other special points. Note that you can visit a special point more than once.
constraints: 2 <= Sum of N over all test cases <= 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T, denoting the number of test cases. The description of each testcase follows. The first line of each testcase contains a single integer, N, which denotes the number of special points. The i-th of the next N lines contains two integers, xi and yi, which denotes that (xi, yi) is a special point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the largest integer D, such that we can start at some special point and visit each of the other special points, directly or through other special points.

sampleTestCases.explanation: We can start at some point and travel to all of the points if D >= 9, but not if D is greater than 9. Hence the answer is 9.
We can start at some point and travel to all of the points if D >= 2, but not if D is greater than 2. Hence the answer is 2.
We can start at some point and travel to all of the points if D >= 10, but not if D is greater than 10. Hence the answer is 10.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. We start by calculating the distance between each pair of points. Then, we sort the distances in descending order. We initialize the maximum distance D to be the largest distance between any two points. We then try to visit all points using this D. If we can visit all points, we return D. Otherwise, we decrease D by 1 and repeat the process until we can visit all points.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2 * log(N)) — The time complexity is dominated by the sorting of distances between points.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — The space complexity is dominated by the storage of distances between points.
